RHB Bank Berhad Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
RHB Bank Berhad reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported net interest income was MYR 965.2 million compared to MYR 996.79 million a year ago. Net income was MYR 904.01 million compared to MYR 833.19 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.2073 compared to MYR 0.1911 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.2066 compared to MYR 0.1905 a year ago.
For the nine months, net interest income was MYR 2,888.02 million compared to MYR 2,912.12 million a year ago. Net income was MYR 2,457.54 million compared to MYR 2,285.67 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.5636 compared to MYR 0.5287 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.5619 compared to MYR 0.5271 a year ago.

data.title: OSK Holdings Berhad Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
OSK Holdings Berhad reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported revenue was MYR 496.68 million compared to MYR 481 million a year ago. Net income was MYR 152.18 million compared to MYR 137.24 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.0492 compared to MYR 0.0444 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.0492 compared to MYR 0.0444 a year ago.
For the nine months, revenue was MYR 1,407.88 million compared to MYR 1,217.37 million a year ago. Net income was MYR 418.47 million compared to MYR 388.87 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.1353 compared to MYR 0.1257 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.1353 compared to MYR 0.1257 a year ago.

data.title: Axiata Group Berhad Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Axiata Group Berhad reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was MYR 2,920.48 million compared to MYR 3,065 million a year ago. Net loss was MYR 27.35 million compared to net income of MYR 976.67 million a year ago. Basic loss per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.003 compared to basic earnings per share from continuing operations of MYR 0.094 a year ago. Diluted loss per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.003 compared to diluted earnings per share from continuing operations of MYR 0.094 a year ago. Basic loss per share was MYR 0.003 compared to basic earnings per share of MYR 0.106 a year ago. Diluted loss per share was MYR 0.003 compared to diluted earnings per share of MYR 0.106 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was MYR 8,778.76 million compared to MYR 9,573.74 million a year ago. Net income was MYR 403.3 million compared to MYR 1,171.6 million a year ago. Basic loss per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.006 compared to basic earnings per share from continuing operations of MYR 0.087 a year ago. Diluted loss per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.006 compared to diluted earnings per share from continuing operations of MYR 0.086 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was MYR 0.044 compared to MYR 0.128 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was MYR 0.044 compared to MYR 0.127 a year ago.

Wärtsilä Signs Three-Year Agreement with SOMELEC in Mauritania
data.description:
Finnish industrial giant Wärtsilä has entered into a three-year Guaranteed Asset Performance agreement with Mauritania's state-owned electricity company SOMELEC for the power plant in Nouadhibou.

The 34-megawatt facility, equipped with two Wärtsilä 32 and two Wärtsilä 46 engines, is vital for ensuring a stable electricity supply in the region.

This new agreement expands the previous collaboration, moving from technical advisory services to full performance responsibility, and was booked in the third quarter of 2025.

No financial details have been disclosed.

Poste Italiane Nears Acquisition of PagoPa for EUR500 Million
data.description:
(Alliance News) - The Italian State Mint and Polygraphic Institute (Istituto Poligrafico e Zecca dello Stato) with a 51% stake, together with Poste Italiane Spa holding the remaining 49%, are reportedly on the verge of acquiring PagoPa. PagoPa is the company controlled by the Ministry of Economy and Finance (Mef) that manages several digital services and platforms, including the Io App. This was reported by Repubblica in the Economics section of its Thursday edition.

According to Bloomberg, the company is valued at EUR500 million: EUR400 million will be paid at the time of acquisition, while the remaining amount will be paid over time.

Since 2016, PagoPA has processed 1.85 billion transactions for a total value of EUR392.6 billion.

By Michele Cirulli, Alliance News Reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

JPMorganChase Announces Intention to Build a New Three-Million Sq Ft Landmark Tower in London
data.description:
JPMorganChase announced its intention to build a new three-million square feet tower in London. This transformative project would enable additional capacity for the firm to grow by creating a world-class workplace for up to 12,000 employees, further strengthening London's position as a global financial hub. The plans are subject to a continuing positive business environment in the UK and the receipt of the necessary approvals and agreements at a national and local level.

Serving as the firm?s principal headquarters in the UK and its most significant presence in EMEA, the building would be situated on the Riverside development in Canary Wharf. The plans will provide employees and clients with a first-class working environment against a backdrop of uninterrupted views across the River Thames to central London. The building is being designed by British architects, Foster + Partners, who also designed the firm?s iconic global headquarters at 270 Park Avenue in New York City.

Its construction is expected to take six years and will begin as soon as necessary approvals and agreements are in place. Canary Wharf Group is working as co-developer on the project, and the firm is being advised independently by Sir George Iacobescu.

data.title: Sime Darby Berhad Reports Earnings Results for the First Quarter Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Sime Darby Berhad reported earnings results for the first quarter ended September 30, 2025. For the first quarter, the company reported sales was MYR 18,031 million compared to MYR 18,264 million a year ago. Net income was MYR 355 million compared to MYR 800 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.052 compared to MYR 0.116 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was MYR 0.052 compared to MYR 0.117 a year ago.

Asahi Takes Earnings Hit After Cyberattack, Flags Potential Data Leak -- Update
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka


Asahi Group said its Japan business took a hit from a recent cyberattack that may have compromised the personal information of nearly two million people.

The company behind Japan's most popular beer on Thursday said the system disruption caused by the cyberattack had dragged core operating profit from its Japan and East Asia segment for the nine months ended September.

The food-and-beverage giant said personal information of 1.9 million individuals may have also been leaked. The potential data breach affects employees and their family members, as well as people who have contacted its customer-service centers. No credit-card information was exposed, but names, residential addresses, phone numbers and email addresses may have been, the company said.

The cyberattack caused a system failure in late September, resulting in the suspension of order, shipment and call-center operations. The company later said it suffered a ransomware attack. The disruption affected only systems managed in Japan.

Founded in 1889, the company owns beer brands including Asahi Super Dry, Italy's Peroni and Pilsner Urquell from the Czech Republic.

Chief Executive Atsushi Katsuki said it couldn't avoid a short-term impact on its Japan operations, but the company remains confident that its businesses are resilient, underpinned by a robust portfolio of brands.

He said that various computer systems are being reinstated, and that the company doesn't intend to revise its midterm business plans due to the system disruption.

"We are making every effort to achieve full system restoration as quickly as possible, while implementing measures to prevent recurrence and strengthening information security across the group," Katsuki said.

Asahi said that for the first nine months of 2025, profits from its businesses in Europe and Asia-Pacific outside East Asia remained broadly in line with its plans.

The company postponed the announcement of detailed nine-month results as it couldn't determine revenue and profit figures for its Japan and East Asia segment.

It has, however, offered some sales figures for its main domestic subsidiaries. October sales from its brewery unit exceeded 90% of the level in the same month last year, while those from its soft drinks subsidiary reached roughly 60% and those of its food unit surpassed 70%.

Asahi said its earnings announcement for 2025 will be delayed beyond the 50 days following the end of the year. The company said it will announce a date for disclosing the annual results once it is determined.


Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-27-25 0255ET

South Korea imposes sanctions on Prince Group and others over online crimes in Southeast Asia
data.description:
SEOUL (Reuters) -South Korea has imposed sanctions on 15 individuals and 132 entities, including Prince Group and those tied to the Southeast Asia-based multinational network, over online crimes in Southeast Asia, the government said on Thursday.

"It is our first unilateral sanction responding to a trans-national crime and the single biggest, which shows the government's willingness to actively respond to online crimes in Southeast Asia," it said in a statement.

South Korea agreed with Cambodia to cooperate on fighting online scams, after repatriating last month more than 60 of its nationals detained in the Southeast Asian country over allegations they were involved in online crimes, following the death of a South Korean student.

Prince Group, which has been accused of operating large-scale online scam centres that used trafficked workers to defraud victims around the world, was sanctioned last month by Britain and the United States.

Authorities across Asia, from Singapore to Taiwan and Hong Kong, have also seized assets or detained individuals tied to the group.

(Reporting by Jihoon LeeEditing by Ed Davies)
